study_cohort_level_records.population_description: Patients categorized by Sepsis-2 and Sepsis-3 definitions, with lactate levels analyzed.
study_cohort_level_records.cohort_characteristics: Lactate levels according to sepsis categories; stratified according to survival.

predictor_model_level_records.effect_size_performance_and_significance: Kruskal-Wallis’ test P < 0.001 among the three sepsis categories. Mann-Whitney’s P < 0.05 post-hoc analysis vs. other categories.
Kruskal-Wallis’ test P < 0.001 among the three sepsis categories. Mann-Whitney’s P < 0.05 post-hoc analysis vs. other categories.

predictor_model_level_records.predictors: Sepsis-2 categories; Lactate concentration (> 2 mmol/L, > 4 mmol/L)
predictor_model_level_records.effect_size_performance_and_significance: Pearson’s Chi-squared test P < 0.001 among the three sepsis categories (same lactate level). Pearson’s Chi-squared test P < 0.05 post-hoc analysis vs. severe sepsis (same lactate level) category. Pearson’s Chi-squared test P < 0.001 among the three lactate categories (same sepsis category level). Pearson’s Chi-squared test P < 0.05 post-hoc analysis vs. whole group and Lactate > 2 mmol/L (same sepsis category level) categories.

predictor_model_level_records.predictors: Sepsis-3 categories; Lactate concentration (> 2 mmol/L, > 4 mmol/L)
predictor_model_level_records.effect_size_performance_and_significance: Pearson’s Chi-squared test P < 0.001 among the three sepsis categories (same lactate level). Pearson’s Chi-squared test P < 0.05 post-hoc analysis vs. sepsis (same lactate level) category. Pearson’s Chi-squared test P < 0.001 among the three lactate categories (same sepsis category level). Pearson’s Chi-squared test P < 0.05 post-hoc analysis vs. whole group and Lactate > 2 mmol/L (same sepsis category level) categories.
